headlines.title: Americans who get Ebola will go to Europe for treatment, not U.S., officials say
headlines.description: If additional Americans contract Ebola and need medical care, they will be sent to Europe rather than brought home to the U.S., senior administration officials said.

headlines.title: L.A. mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt accuses Karen Bass of 'criminal negligence'
headlines.description: Los Angeles mayoral candidate and former reality TV star Spencer Pratt sits down with NBC News' Tom Llamas to discuss his campaign, in which he accuses Mayor Karen Bass and Governor Gavin Newsom of "criminal negligence" over their handling of the devastating L.A. wildfires.

headlines.title: Farmers in Iowa are struggling in Trump’s economy, but many say they still support him
headlines.description: 13 farmers across Iowa said they’re struggling economically, but 11 voted for Trump in the past and still largely back him, hoping “he follows through” on his promises.

headlines.title: In major shake-up, CBS replaces ‘60 Minutes’ executive producer with tech journalist
headlines.description: CBS News editor Bari Weiss hired a new executive producer at “60 Minutes,” the network’s flagship newsmagazine: Nick Bilton, a former New York Times tech columnist.

headlines.title: Houston planning out-of-this-world experience for World Cup fans this summer
headlines.description: Soccer fans headed to Houston for the World Cup this summer can expect an out-of-this-world experience as the city combines its connection to the stars while roping in some of the best of what the city has to offer by keeping two feet on the ground.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Feds say ex-NBA player Terry Rozier accepted $100K bribe in sports betting scheme
headlines.description: Former NBA player Terry Rozier was hit with new federal charges of sports bribery and honest services wire fraud conspiracy in a massive sports betting investigation that has rocked the league.

headlines.title: Student commencement boos are a sign of wider AI woes
headlines.description: As companies race to weave AI into nearly every industry, some college students are responding with open hostility

headlines.title: Trump administration prepares for proposed $250 bill with the president’s face on it
headlines.description: The Treasury Department is preparing to print $250 bills with President Donald Trump’s face on them and is just waiting for Congress’ green light, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Thursday

headlines.title: Teen ‘takeovers’ push cities to take action as experts see echoes of the Covid pandemic
headlines.description: Teenagers behaving badly in groups is hardly a new thing, experts said. But social media has made it easier to rapidly mobilize this generation of teenagers, who may be craving social interaction and looking for an outlet after having spent their formative years isolated during the Covid pandemic.

headlines.title: Spencer Pratt says he doesn’t need ‘anyone’s endorsement’ after Trump backs L.A. mayor bid
headlines.description: Former reality TV star and Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt rebuffed a potential endorsement from President Donald Trump, telling NBC News on Thursday, “I don’t need anyone’s endorsement but mothers’.

headlines.title: An unknown number of people killed in gas explosion at Dallas apartment building
headlines.description: An unknown number of people were killed and at least four people were hospitalized after a gas explosion destroyed a Dallas apartment complex Thursday afternoon, city officials said
